edge_hypothesis: EMA crossover has 0 ACCEPTs across 600+ pipeline runs — but it has NEVER been tested with proper R:R. The pipeline tested it at 1.3-3:1 R:R where HyperLiquid fees destroy any edge. Every single ACCEPT (23/23) uses 5:1+ R:R. The EMA 9/21 crossover is the simplest possible trend-shift signal: it fires frequently, catching every genuine trend start AND every ranging oscillation. At 8:1 R:R (2.0x stop, 16.0x TP), most entries get stopped out quickly (cheap probes), but the ~12-15% that catch a genuine trend continuation produce outsized returns. The crossover is specifically suited to ranging markets where EMA_9 and EMA_21 oscillate around each other — exactly the regime where 19/23 ACCEPTs profit. If ema_crossover fails at 8:1 R:R, the template is genuinely dead and should be removed from the registry. If it succeeds, it proves the advisory thesis: the problem was always R:R, never the signal. Invalidation: PF < 1.0 at 8:1 R:R on ETH/4h = template is structural noise.

variants.name: ema_xover_tail_scout_eth_8to1
variants.description: EMA 9/21 crossover on ETH/4h with 8:1 R:R tail harvesting. The simplest signal at extreme R:R. EMA_9 crossing above EMA_21 = long, crossing below = short. No additional filters — pure crossover signal. The hypothesis: simplicity + extreme R:R > complexity + moderate R:R. At 2.0x ATR stop, false signals are cheap. At 16.0x ATR TP, the rare trend continuation pays 8x the risk. Expected: 100-200 signals over backtest period, ~15% hit rate needed for PF > 1.0.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (16.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < 1.0 at 8:1 R:R, ema_crossover is structural noise — remove from registry
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 16.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_9 / EMA_21 crossover — simplest trend-shift signal, fires frequently in ranging
EMA_9 cross of EMA_21 IS the signal — no secondary confirmation needed
ATR_14 for 2.0x stop / 16.0x TP — 8:1 R:R tail harvesting

variants.name: ema_xover_tail_scout_eth_1h_6to1
variants.description: EMA 9/21 crossover on ETH/1h with 6:1 R:R. Higher frequency variant. 1h timeframe should produce 3-4x more crossover signals than 4h, giving better sample size (expected 300-500 signals). 6:1 R:R is slightly more conservative (1.5x ATR stop, 9.0x ATR TP) to account for 1h noise. If 1h crossover works at 6:1 but 4h needs 8:1, it confirms that timeframe affects optimal R:R calibration.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (9.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < 1.0 on 1h at 6:1, crossover noise on 1h exceeds signal quality
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 9.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_9 / EMA_21 crossover on 1h — higher frequency, more signals
Cross signal only — testing if raw simplicity works at proper R:R
ATR_14 for 1.5x stop / 9.0x TP — 6:1 R:R, tighter for 1h noise
